options.content:
## CI-CD / Microservices Overview — read me first

Pick the tier in **`deployment_environment`** (stable | develop) and narrow with **`service_name`**.

| | **stable** | **develop** |
|---|---|---|
| Source | `main` branch → `microservices` ns | `develop` branch → `microservices-develop` ns |
| Topology | full (HA DBs) | lean (single-instance DBs) |

### Signals on this board
- **HTTP RED** (`http_server_*`) — **R**ate, **E**rrors (4xx/5xx), **D**uration (p50/p95/p99), per service & route.
- **Span-metrics RED** (`traces_spanmetrics_*`) — RED derived from traces, per service / operation / span-kind.
- **Service dependency graph** (`traces_service_graph_*`) — service→service edges (rate / failed / latency). ⚠️ This metric has **no `deployment_environment` label**, so its panels show **all tiers combined**.
- **JVM & runtime** — heap / GC / threads / CPU **summary**; full depth in the **CI-CD JVM internals** dashboard.

### Why a panel may show no data
- 🟢 **`0` = healthy** — error rates and GC pause sit at 0 when nothing is wrong / the service is idle.
- 📈 **Needs traffic** — every RED panel (HTTP + span-metrics) is rate-based, so an idle service draws a flat 0 line. Generate load with the **`Day2.traffic.01-k6`** workflow, then the request-rate / latency / error panels come alive.
- 🌐 **Service graph = all tiers** — `traces_service_graph_*` carries no `deployment_environment` label, so that section is **not** filtered by the tier picker.

### ⚠️ Free tier vs paid — the *Kubernetes pod & container infra* section
`config.yaml → observability.grafanaCloudTier` defaults to **`free`**, which turns on **`leanMetrics`**: the collector **drops the cluster-infra metrics** (kube-state-metrics + cAdvisor: `kube_pod_*`, `kube_deployment_*`, `container_cpu_*`, `container_memory_*`) to stay under the **free-tier 15k active-series cap**. Those series simply aren't ingested, so the **Kubernetes pod & container infra** section below is **empty on the free tier — by design, not a fault**.

Everything else on this board comes from **app/OTel metrics** (`http_server_*`, `traces_*`, `jvm_*`) which are **always** shipped, free tier or not. To light up the infra section, switch to **paid** (`grafanaCloudTier: paid`, or `leanMetrics: false`) and re-run `Day1.cluster.01-gke` / `Day2.redeploy.01-argocd`. The panels are kept here intentionally so the board is ready the moment you upgrade.

panels.title: Service dependency graph (all tiers — no deployment_environment label)
JVM & runtime (summary — deep dive in 'CI-CD JVM internals')
JVM memory used by service (heap + non-heap)
Kubernetes pod & container infra — EMPTY on free tier (needs leanMetrics=false / paid)
Pod restarts (rate)
panels.description: Requires kube-state-metrics + cAdvisor, which leanMetrics drops on the free tier. Populates when grafanaCloudTier=paid / leanMetrics=false.

panels.title: Container memory (working set)
panels.description: Requires kube-state-metrics + cAdvisor, which leanMetrics drops on the free tier. Populates when grafanaCloudTier=paid / leanMetrics=false.

panels.title: Container CPU (cores)
panels.description: Requires kube-state-metrics + cAdvisor, which leanMetrics drops on the free tier. Populates when grafanaCloudTier=paid / leanMetrics=false.

panels.title: Deployment replicas (desired vs available)
panels.description: Requires kube-state-metrics + cAdvisor, which leanMetrics drops on the free tier. Populates when grafanaCloudTier=paid / leanMetrics=false.

panels.title: Pods by phase
panels.description: Requires kube-state-metrics + cAdvisor, which leanMetrics drops on the free tier. Populates when grafanaCloudTier=paid / leanMetrics=false.

panels.title: Microservices pod logs — $deployment_environment
panels.description: Filter by $level. trace_id in a line links to the trace (logs→traces via derived fields).

targets.expr: {k8s_namespace_name="$namespace", service_name=~"$service_name"} | detected_level=~"$level" | json | line_format "{{ if .message }}{{ .message }}{{ else if .msg }}{{ .msg }}{{ else }}{{ __line__ }}{{ end }}"

panels.title: Recent traces — $service_name
panels.description: Click a Trace ID → trace view (links to logs & metrics via the Tempo datasource config).

list.name: level
list.label: Log level
list.description: Filter the logs panel by severity (detected_level). All includes plain-text (unknown).
